description: Creates a Bayes' theorem computation table. Define hypotheses with priors using hypothesis() and likelihoods using likelihood(). Joint probabilities and posteriors are computed automatically. A total row shows the sum of joints.
utility: Create a Bayes' theorem computation table

arguments.name: hypotheses
arguments.description: One or more hypothesis() definitions with labels and prior probabilities

arguments.name: likelihoods
arguments.description: One likelihood() per hypothesis (in order), each specifying P(A|E_i)

examples.scenario: Classic bag problem: P(Bag II | Red ball drawn)
examples.expression:
BT = bayestable(at(4,10),
    hypothesis("Bag I", probability(1, 2)),
    hypothesis("Bag II", probability(1, 2)),
    likelihood(probability(3, 7)),
    likelihood(probability(5, 11)))

BT = bayestable(at(4,10),
    hypothesis("Machine A", probability(25, 100)),
    hypothesis("Machine B", probability(35, 100)),
    hypothesis("Machine C", probability(40, 100)),
    likelihood(probability(5, 100)),
    likelihood(probability(4, 100)),
    likelihood(probability(2, 100)))

BT = bayestable(at(4,10),
    hypothesis("Has HIV", probability(1, 1000)),
    hypothesis("No HIV", probability(999, 1000)),
    likelihood(probability(9, 10)),
    likelihood(probability(1, 100)))
